What is quantum entanglement? 🔬

Quantum entanglement is a phenomenon where two or more particles become correlated in such a way that the quantum state of each particle cannot be described independently.1

  • Non-local — correlation persists regardless of distance2
  • Instantaneous — measuring one particle instantly affects the other
  • No faster-than-light communication — cannot transmit information1
from qiskit import QuantumCircuit

# Create a Bell state (maximally entangled pair)
qc = QuantumCircuit(2)
qc.h(0)    # Hadamard gate on qubit 0
qc.cx(0, 1)  # CNOT: entangle qubit 0 and 1
print(qc)
Can entanglement be used for teleportation? 🚀

Quantum teleportation is real but often misunderstood. It teleports quantum state, not matter or energy.

The process requires a classical communication channel alongside the entangled pair, making it impossible to use for faster-than-light signalling.
